choiceresponse.label: Check the Lime explainer output for the first sentence: “Hi Alex, are we still on for our meeting tomorrow at 10? No rush—just confirming.” Which statements are supported by the LIME explanation? (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: Words like “meeting” and “tomorrow” contribute toward the ham prediction.
The model relies primarily on sentence tone (politeness and informality), which LIME captures through words like “Hi” and “just.”
The model classifies this as ham primarily because it contains a person’s name.
LIME shows that no words in the sentence contribute to spam.
This prediction implies that all messages about meetings are non-spam.

div.p: LIME highlights words such as meeting and tomorrow as contributing toward the ham class. It also shows that no words contribute positively toward the spam class for this sentence. Although conversational tokens like Hi appear in the explanation, LIME does not support the claim that the model is reasoning primarily about tone or politeness; it only shows token-level contributions. As with all LIME explanations, this interpretation is local and applies only to this specific message.
